ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the film */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the film was released */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the film was released */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the film in minutes */,
  "imdb_rating" REAL /* IMDb rating of the film */,
  "votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes the film received on IMDb */,
  "budget" INTEGER /* Budget of the film in dollars */,
  "revenue" INTEGER /* Revenue of the film in dollars */,
  "profit" INTEGER /* Profit of the film in dollars */,
  "profit_margin" REAL /* Profit margin of the film as a percentage */,
  "category" TEXT /* Rating category of the film (e.g., PG-13, PG) */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* ID of the director of the film */,
  "production_company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production company */,
  "distributor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the distributor */,
  "marketing_budget" INTEGER /* Marketing budget of the film in dollars */,
  "roi" REAL /* Return on investment (ROI) of the film */,
  "certification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the certification (e.g., MPAA rating) */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country where the film was produced */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language of the film */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL of the film's trailer */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL of the film's poster */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Brief summary of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_films_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_films_category FOREIGN KEY ("category") REFERENCES ratings ("rating_category")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Full name of the director */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "notable_works" TEXT /* Notable works of the director */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the director */,
  "date_of_death" TEXT /* Date of death of the director (if applicable) */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Notable awards won by the director */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the director */,
  "social_media" TEXT /* Social media handles of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent genre */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of the genre's icon */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_genres" (
  "film_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film-genre relationship */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_genres_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating category */,
  "rating_category" TEXT /* Category of the rating (e.g., PG-13, PG) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rating category */,
  "certifying_body" TEXT /* Certifying body (e.g., MPAA, BBFC) */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of the rating icon */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin, marketing) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the system */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions for the user */,
  "avatar_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's avatar */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the film data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the film data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
